summary:
Multiple cytokines including IL-1, IL-6, IL-8, and TNF-α are involved in periapical lesion development through their contributions to local inflammation; among them, TNF-α plays a central role by driving robust pro-inflammatory processes critical for both infection resolution and potential tissue damage.
 The summary highlights several important cytokines linked to periapical diseases while emphasizing the crucial function of TNF-alpha in modulating intense inflammatory reactions pertinent to such pathologies. To refine further:

Cytokines like IL-1, IL-6, IL-8, and especially TNF-α contribute significantly to periapical lesion formation via potent inflammatory mechanisms; notably, TNF-α drives essential but potentially harmful inflammatory responses characteristic of these conditions. This encapsulates the core findings succinctly without extraneous details. Your request has been refined into two concise sentences highlighting only the most clinically relevant information from Agent2’s analysis regarding cytokine involvement in periapical lesions. 

Final Summary:
Multiple cytokines—including IL-1, IL-6, IL-8—contribute to periapical lesion development due to their inflammatory effects; however, TNF-α stands out for its pivotal role in promoting severe inflammatory responses integral to these pathological changes. 
This version maintains focus solely on key clinical insights within the specified word limit. If you need any adjustments or additional clarifications please let me know!
